image_requests.prompt: Ancient ruins of Mohenjo-daro Indus Valley civilization, wide cinematic establishing shot, crumbling weathered ochre brick walls and platforms spreading across a dusty arid plain, overcast or hazy golden-hour diffused light, atmospheric dust haze on the horizon, no people, desolate and ancient, dreamlike quality, photorealistic, 16:9 landscape format
image_requests.purpose: Full-bleed hero background — the visual subject of the thumbnail, communicating 'ancient lost civilization' the instant the viewer's eye lands on the frame

html: JUSTVANISHED
why: The two-line typographic split is the core decision: 'JUST' at 292px hits the viewer first like a blunt statement, then 'VANISHED' at 187px lands as the gut-punch completion — together they scale to 37px and 24px at 168px thumbnail width, both readable on a phone screen. The ruins image is desaturated to ~52% and tinted with a cool indigo wash to feel ancient and dreamlike rather than documentary, while a faint warm amber radial glow at the center lets the ochre brick bleed through — the civilization still glows faintly, as if memory hasn't fully faded. The bottom-rising gradient creates a stage of darkness for the text without obscuring the ruins in the upper two-thirds of the frame, so the viewer sees crumbling brick city above and two haunting words below — topic and emotion decoded in under a second.
title: Boring History for Sleep: The Mystery of the Indus Valley Civilization
